sentences.zh: 」列人，縣，故城在今洺州肥郷縣東北。〉

translations.literal: ' Lieren is a county; its old city is northeast of Feixiang county in Mingzhou today.>
translations.idiomatic: Lieren lay northeast of present-day Feixiang in southern Hebei.>

sentences.zh: 光武不荅，去之真定。

translations.literal: Guangwu did not answer and left for Zhending.
translations.idiomatic: He gave no reply and rode on toward Zhending,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬真定國，今恒州縣也。〉

translations.literal: <A county name, belonging to the principality of Zhending; it is modern Hengzhou county.>
translations.idiomatic: <Zhending county in the Zhending princedom—modern Zhengding near Shijiazhuang.>

sentences.zh: 林於是乃詐以卜者王郎為成帝子子輿，

translations.literal: Lin thereupon falsely represented the diviner Wang Lang as Chengdi's son Ziyu,
translations.idiomatic: Lin then spread the fraud that the fortune-teller Wang Lang was Emperor Cheng's lost son Ziyu,

sentences.zh: 〈《前書》曰，立國將軍孫建奏云「不知何一男子遮臣車前，自稱漢氏劉子輿，成帝下妻子也，劉氏當復」。

translations.literal: <The Former Han History says the Establish the Nation general Sun Jian memorialized: 'Some unknown man blocked my chariot, calling himself the Han clansman Liu Ziyu, Chengdi's surviving son; the Liu house is due to rise again.'
translations.idiomatic: <The Han shu cites General Sun Jian's memorial about a stranger who blocked his carriage claiming to be Liu Ziyu, Chengdi's posthumous heir,

sentences.zh: 故郎因而稱之。〉

translations.literal: Therefore Lang adopted that identity.>
translations.idiomatic: and Wang Lang simply recycled the imposture.>

sentences.zh: 十二月，立郎為天子，都邯鄲，遂遣使者降下郡國。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month they enthroned Lang as Son of Heaven with capital at Handan, then sent envoys to bring commanderies and principalities to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: That winter they crowned Wang Lang at Handan and issued edicts demanding every circuit submit.

sentences.zh: 二年正月，光武以王郎新盛，乃北徇薊。

translations.literal: In the first month of the second year, because Wang Lang was newly powerful, Guangwu marched north to seize Ji.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the second Gengshi year, as Wang Lang's power peaked, Guangwu struck north for Ji county,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬涿郡，今幽州縣也。

translations.literal: <A county name, belonging to Zhuo commandery; it is modern Youzhou county.
translations.idiomatic: <Ji lay in Zhuo commandery—present-day Ji county on Beijing's southwest flank.

sentences.zh: 本字從「契」從「邑」，見說文。〉

translations.literal: The original character combines 'contract' with 'settlement'; see the Shuowen.>
translations.idiomatic: The orthography couples 'bond' with the place radical, per the Shuowen.>

sentences.zh: 王郎移檄購光武十萬戶，

translations.literal: Wang Lang circulated a summons offering one hundred thousand households for Guangwu's capture,
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lang's proclamation promised a fief of a hundred thousand households to whoever seized him,

sentences.zh: 〈《說文》曰：「檄，以木簡為書，長尺二寸。

translations.literal: <The Shuowen says: 'Xi is a document on a wooden slip one chi two cun long.
translations.idiomatic: <A xi proclamation was a foot-long wooden slip,

sentences.zh: 謂之檄，以徵召也。

translations.literal: It is called xi because it summons.
translations.idiomatic: named from its use in summonses,

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「以財有所求曰購。

translations.literal: ' It also says: 'To seek something with a reward is called gou (purchase).'
translations.idiomatic: and gou meant a posted reward.

sentences.zh: 」魏武奏事曰：「若有急，即插以雞羽，謂之羽檄。」〉

translations.literal: ' Wei Wu's memorial affairs says: 'If there is urgency, then insert chicken feathers—this is called a feather xi.'>
translations.idiomatic: Cao Cao's office rules add that urgent despatches carried a chicken feather.>

sentences.zh: 而故廣陽王子劉接

translations.literal: while former Prince of Guangyang's son Liu Jie
translations.idiomatic: while Liu Jie, son of the late Prince of Guangyang,

sentences.zh: 〈廣陽王名嘉，武帝五代孫。〉

translations.literal: <The Prince of Guangyang was named Jia, fifth-generation descendant of Wudi.>
translations.idiomatic: <The prince's name was Jia, five generations down from Emperor Wu.>

sentences.zh: 起兵薊中以應郎，城內擾亂，轉相驚恐，言邯鄲使者方到，二千石以下皆出迎。

translations.literal: raised troops inside Ji to respond to Lang; inside the city there was turmoil; in turn they frightened one another, saying envoys from Handan were about to arrive, and all below two thousand shi went out to welcome them.
translations.idiomatic: rose inside Ji for Wang Lang. Panic spread through the garrison: rumor said Handan's heralds were at the gates, and every official under two thousand shi rushed out to greet them.

sentences.zh: 於是光武趣駕南轅，

translations.literal: Thereupon Guangwu hastily drove the chariot with the yoke turned south,
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu whipped his carriage about and fled south,

sentences.zh: 〈趣，急也，讀曰促。〉

translations.literal: <Qu means urgent; read it like cu (hasten).>
translations.idiomatic: <The graph here means 'hurry' and is read like cu.>

sentences.zh: 晨夜不敢入城邑，舍食道傍。

translations.literal: morning and night he did not dare enter cities or towns, but lodged and ate beside the road.
translations.idiomatic: too frightened to enter walled towns by day or night; the column slept and ate in the ditches.

sentences.zh: 至饒陽，

translations.literal: He reached Raoyang,
translations.idiomatic: They reached Raoyang,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬安平國，在饒河之陽，故城在今瀛州饒陽縣東北。〉

translations.literal: <A county name, belonging to the principality of Anping; it lies on the north bank of the Rao River; its old city is northeast of Raoyang county in Yingzhou today.>
translations.idiomatic: <Raoyang in Anping princedom—north of the Rao River, northeast of the modern county of the same name in Hebei.>

sentences.zh: 官屬皆乏食。

translations.literal: The staff all lacked food.
translations.idiomatic: His followers were starving.

sentences.zh: 光武乃自稱邯鄲使者，入傳舍。

translations.literal: Guangwu therefore called himself an envoy from Handan and entered the relay inn.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu bluffed his way into the post-house as a courier from Handan,

sentences.zh: 〈客館也。

translations.literal: <A travelers' hostel.
translations.idiomatic: <That is, the government hostel for traveling officials.

sentences.zh: 傳音知戀反，下同。〉

translations.literal: Chuan is read zhi-lian fan; the same below.>
translations.idiomatic: The commentary gives the fanqie zhi-lian for chuan, here meaning relay station.>

sentences.zh: 傳吏方進食，從者飢，爭奪之。

translations.literal: The relay clerk was just bringing food; the followers were hungry and snatched it.
translations.idiomatic: The postmaster was laying out a meal; the famished escort fell on the trays,

sentences.zh: 傳吏疑其偽，乃椎鼓數十通，

translations.literal: The relay clerk suspected they were fake and therefore beat the drum several tens of times,
translations.idiomatic: which convinced the clerk he had impostors—he began pounding the alarm drum,

sentences.zh: 〈椎音直追反。〉

translations.literal: <Chui is read zhi-zhui fan.>
translations.idiomatic: <Chui 'to pound' is read zhi-zhui.>

sentences.zh: 紿言邯鄲將軍至，

translations.literal: falsely announcing the Handan general had arrived,
translations.idiomatic: shouting that the Handan general had come,

sentences.zh: 〈紿，言欺誑也，音殆。〉

translations.literal: <Dai means to deceive; read like dai (peril).>
translations.idiomatic: <Dai here means to lie, read like the dai of 'peril.'>

sentences.zh: 官屬皆失色。

translations.literal: the staff all changed color.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu's officers went white.

sentences.zh: 光武升車欲馳；

translations.literal: Guangwu mounted the chariot intending to gallop off;
translations.idiomatic: He leapt for his carriage to bolt,

sentences.zh: 旣而懼不免，徐還坐，曰：「請邯鄲將軍入。

translations.literal: then, fearing he could not escape, slowly returned and sat, saying: 'Invite the Handan general in.'
translations.idiomatic: then thought better of it, sauntered back to his seat, and drawled, 'Show the general in.'

sentences.zh: 」乆乃駕去。

translations.literal: ' After a long while he drove away.
translations.idiomatic: A long, tense pause later he cantered off.

sentences.zh: 傳中人遙語門者閉之。

translations.literal: Someone inside the relay station shouted from afar to the gatekeeper to shut it.
translations.idiomatic: A voice from the hostel yard ordered the gates barred,

sentences.zh: 門長曰：「天下詎可知，而閉長者乎？

translations.literal: The gate captain said: 'How can the empire be known for certain, and you would shut out your elders?'
translations.idiomatic: but the gate captain retorted, 'Who can read fate well enough to lock out a great man?'

sentences.zh: 」遂得南出。

translations.literal: ' Therefore he was able to go out south.
translations.idiomatic: So the gates stayed open and Guangwu slipped south.

sentences.zh: 晨夜兼行，蒙犯霜雪，

translations.literal: They traveled morning and night together, braving frost and snow,
translations.idiomatic: They doubled day and night through ice and sleet,

sentences.zh: 〈蒙，冒也。〉

translations.literal: <Meng means to brave.
translations.idiomatic: <Meng means to face into,

sentences.zh: 天時寒，面皆破裂。

translations.literal: the weather was cold; everyone's faces cracked.
translations.idiomatic: until the cold split their cheeks raw.

sentences.zh: 至呼沱河，

translations.literal: They reached the Hutuo River,
translations.idiomatic: They came to the Hutuo River,

sentences.zh: 〈《山海經》云：「太戲之山，滹沱之水出焉。

translations.literal: <The Classic of Mountains and Seas says: 'On Mount Taixi the Hutuo water issues.'
translations.idiomatic: <The Shan hai jing places the source on Mount Taixi,

sentences.zh: 」在今代州繁畤縣東，流經定州深澤縣東南，即光武所度處，今俗猶謂之危度口。

translations.literal: ' It is east of Fanzhi county in Daizhou today, flows southeast past the southeast of Shenze county in Dingzhou—this is where Guangwu crossed; popular speech still calls it the Danger Ford mouth.
translations.idiomatic: east of Fanzhi in Shanxi, then southeast past Shenze—this was Guangwu's crossing, still remembered as the 'peril ford.'

sentences.zh: 臣賢案：呼沱河舊在饒陽南，至魏太祖曹操因饒河故瀆決，令北注新溝水，所以今在饒陽縣北。〉

translations.literal: Your servant Xian notes: the old Hutuo lay south of Raoyang; down to Wei Taizu Cao Cao it was diverted through the old Rao channel northward into the new channel water; therefore today it is north of Raoyang county.>
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian adds that the river shifted north after Cao Cao cut new channels, which is why it now lies north of Raoyang.>

sentences.zh: 無船，適遇冰合，得過，

translations.literal: There were no boats; it happened that ice joined and they were able to cross,
translations.idiomatic: There was no ferry; the surface froze solid just in time,

sentences.zh: 〈《續漢書》曰：「時冰滑馬僵，乃各以囊盛沙，布冰上度焉。」〉

translations.literal: <The Continued Han History says: 'At that time the ice was slippery and the horses fell; they each filled sacks with sand and spread it on the ice to cross.'>
translations.idiomatic: The Xu Han shu says men spread sand from sacks on the slick ice to get the horses across.>

sentences.zh: 未畢數車而陷。

translations.literal: before several carts had finished they broke through.
translations.idiomatic: Only the last wagons broke through before they cleared the span.

sentences.zh: 進至下博城西，

translations.literal: He advanced to the west of Xiabo city,
translations.idiomatic: They pushed on to the west side of Xiabo,

sentences.zh: 〈下博，縣，屬信都國。

translations.literal: <Xiabo is a county, belonging to the principality of Xindu;
translations.idiomatic: <Xiabo county in Xindu princedom,

sentences.zh: 在博水之下，故曰下博。

translations.literal: it lies below the Bo waters, therefore it is called Lower Bo.
translations.idiomatic: so named because it sits downstream on the Bo River,

sentences.zh: 故城在今兾州下博縣南。〉

translations.literal: its old city is south of Xiabo county in Jizhou today.>
translations.idiomatic: the ruins lying south of the modern Hebei county.>

sentences.zh: 遑惑不知所之。

translations.literal: Confused and alarmed, they did not know where to go.
translations.idiomatic: No one knew which road led to safety.

sentences.zh: 有白衣老父在道旁，

translations.literal: There was an old man in white by the roadside,
translations.idiomatic: An old man in white stood by the track,

sentences.zh: 〈老父蓋神人也，今下博縣西猶有祠堂。〉

translations.literal: <The old father was probably a spirit; west of Xiabo county there is still a shrine today.>
translations.idiomatic: <Tradition calls him a deity; a shrine still marks the spot west of Xiabo.>

sentences.zh: 指曰：「努力！

translations.literal: pointing he said: 'Strive hard!'
translations.idiomatic: He pointed and said, 'Press on!'

sentences.zh: 信都郡為長安守，去此八十里。」

translations.literal: The commandery of Xindu holds out for Chang'an; it is eighty li from here.'"
translations.idiomatic: Xindu commandery still flies Han's banner for Chang'an—only eighty li ahead.'"

sentences.zh: 〈信都郡，今兾州也。〉

translations.literal: <Xindu commandery is modern Jizhou.>
translations.idiomatic: <That is the Xindu circuit around modern Hengshui in Hebei.>

sentences.zh: 光武即馳赴之，信都太守任光開門出迎。

translations.literal: Guangwu immediately galloped thither; the governor of Xindu, Ren Guang, opened the gates and came out to welcome him.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu spurred for Xindu, where Prefect Ren Guang threw open the walls to receive him.

sentences.zh: 世祖因發旁縣，得四千人，先擊堂陽、貰縣，皆降之。

translations.literal: Shizu thereupon mobilized neighboring counties, obtaining four thousand men, first attacked Tangyang and Shi county, and both surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: He then drafted four thousand men from nearby counties, struck Tangyang and Shi, and both places yielded,

sentences.zh: 〈堂陽及貰並屬鉅鹿郡。

translations.literal: <Tangyang and Shi both belonged to Julu commandery.
translations.idiomatic: <Both counties stood in Julu commandery,

sentences.zh: 堂陽在堂水之陽，今兾州縣，故城在今兾州鹿城縣西南。

translations.literal: Tangyang is on the south of the Tang River; it is a Jizhou county today; its old city is southwest of Lucheng county in Jizhou today.
translations.idiomatic: Tangyang south of the Tang stream—southwest of present Lucheng in Hebei,

sentences.zh: 貰音時夜反。〉

translations.literal: Shi is read shi-ye fan.>
translations.idiomatic: Shi is read shi-ye.>

sentences.zh: 王莽和成卒正邳彤亦舉郡降。

translations.literal: Wang Mang's Heqing corps commandant Pi Tong also raised the commandery and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: while Pi Tong, Wang Mang's Heqing garrison commandant, brought his whole commandery over to Han.

sentences.zh: 〈《東觀記》曰：「王莽分鉅鹿為和成郡。

translations.literal: <The Eastern Lodge Record says: 'Wang Mang split Julu into Heqing commandery.'
translations.idiomatic: <The Dongguan ji notes that Mang carved Heqing out of Julu.

sentences.zh: 」卒正，職如太守。〉

translations.literal: ' The corps commandant's duties were like those of a grand administrator.'>
translations.idiomatic: The Heqing 'corps commandant' held powers equivalent to a prefect.>

sentences.zh: 又昌城人劉植，宋子人耿純，

translations.literal: Also Liu Zhi of Changcheng and Geng Chun of Songzi,
translations.idiomatic: Liu Zhi from Changcheng and Geng Chun from Songzi joined him,

sentences.zh: 〈昌城，縣，屬信都國，故城在今兾州西北。

translations.literal: <Changcheng is a county belonging to Xindu principality; its old city is northwest of Jizhou today.
translations.idiomatic: <Changcheng lay in Xindu; the ruins stand northwest of the Hengshui region.

sentences.zh: 宋子，縣，屬鉅鹿郡，故城在今趙州平棘縣北。〉

translations.literal: Songzi is a county belonging to Julu commandery; its old city is north of Pingji county in Zhao prefecture today.>
translations.idiomatic: Songzi was in Julu, north of present Pingxiang in Hebei.>

sentences.zh: 各率宗親子弟，據其縣邑，以奉光武。

translations.literal: each leading clan kin and young men, occupying their county seats, to support Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: each mustering kinsmen to hold his home county for Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 於是北降下曲陽，

translations.literal: Thereupon he subdued Lower Quyang to the north,
translations.idiomatic: He then took Lower Quyang on the march north,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬鉅鹿郡。

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Julu commandery.
translations.idiomatic: <Lower Quyang was a Julu county,

sentences.zh: 常山郡有上曲陽，故此言下。〉

translations.literal: Changshan commandery had Upper Quyang, therefore this is called Lower.'>
translations.idiomatic: distinguished from Upper Quyang in Changshan by the prefix 'Lower.'>

sentences.zh: 衆稍合，樂附者至有數萬人。

translations.literal: The host gradually assembled; those happy to join reached several tens of thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Reinforcements trickled in until glad volunteers numbered in the tens of thousands.

sentences.zh: 復北擊中山，

translations.literal: Again he struck north at Zhongshan,
translations.idiomatic: He next drove into the Zhongshan princedom,

sentences.zh: 〈中山，國，一名中人亭，故城在今定州唐縣東北。

translations.literal: <Zhongshan is a principality, also called Zhongren ting; its old city is northeast of Tang county in Dingzhou today.
translations.idiomatic: <Zhongshan's capital sat northeast of present Tang county in Hebei,

sentences.zh: 張曜中山記曰：「城中有山，故曰中山。」〉

translations.literal: Zhang Yao's Zhongshan Record says: 'There is a hill inside the city wall, therefore it is called Zhongshan.'>
translations.idiomatic: named from an in-town hill per Zhang Yao's local gazetteer.>

sentences.zh: 拔盧奴。

translations.literal: He seized Luunu.
translations.idiomatic: and stormed Luunu.

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬中山國，故城在今定州安喜縣。

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Zhongshan; its old city is at modern Anxi county in Dingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: <Luunu belonged to Zhongshan; the site is near present Anxi,

sentences.zh: 水經注曰：「縣有黑水故池，水黑曰盧，不流曰奴，因以為名。」〉

translations.literal: The Shuijing zhu says: 'The county has the old Black Water pool; water black is lu, not flowing is nu; therefore it is named thus.'>
translations.idiomatic: the Shuijing zhu deriving the name from a stagnant black pool.>

sentences.zh: 所過發奔命兵，

translations.literal: Where he passed he drafted emergency-response troops,
translations.idiomatic: Along the route he impressed 'run-on-signal' militia,

sentences.zh: 〈《前書》音義曰：「舊時郡國皆有材官、騎士，若有急難，權取驍勇者聞命奔赴，故謂之『奔命』。」〉

translations.literal: <The Former Han yin yi says: 'Formerly every commandery had foresters and mounted knights; if there was sudden crisis, they temporarily took the brave and keen who heard the order and ran to respond; therefore they are called run-on-signal.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Han shu yin yi: standing militia were called out in crisis for instant marches—'run-on-signal' levies.>

sentences.zh: 移檄邊部，共擊邯鄲，郡縣還復響應。

translations.literal: He shifted proclamations to the border divisions to jointly strike Handan; commanderies and counties in turn responded.
translations.idiomatic: He sent circulars to the frontier garrisons to coordinate against Handan, and county after county answered.

sentences.zh: 南擊新市、真定、元氏、防子，皆下之，

translations.literal: South he struck Xinshi, Zhending, Yuanshi, and Fangzi; all fell.
translations.idiomatic: A southern sweep took Xinshi, Zhending, Yuanshi, and Fangzi in succession,

sentences.zh: 〈新市，縣，屬鉅鹿郡，故城在今恒州東北。

translations.literal: <Xinshi is a county belonging to Julu commandery; its old city is northeast of Hengzhou today.
translations.idiomatic: <Xinshi in Julu—northeast of the old Hengzhou belt,

sentences.zh: 元氏、房子，屬常山郡，並今趙州縣也。

translations.literal: Yuanshi and Fangzi belong to Changshan commandery; both are Zhao prefecture counties today.
translations.idiomatic: while Yuanshi and Fangzi lay in Changshan, in the southern Zhao plain.

sentences.zh: 防與房古字通用。〉

translations.literal: The graphs for the place name (here written with the 'dike' signific) and the usual 'chamber' graph were interchangeable in antiquity.'>
translations.idiomatic: Editors note that the county name could be spelled with either the 'dike' or the 'chamber' graph.>

sentences.zh: 因入趙界。

translations.literal: Thus he entered Zhao territory.
translations.idiomatic: With that he crossed into the Zhao heartland.

sentences.zh: 時王郎大將李育屯柏人，

translations.literal: At that time Wang Lang's great general Li Yu was camped at Bairen,
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lang's general Li Yu held Bairen,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬趙國，今邢州縣，故城在縣之西北。〉

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to the principality of Zhao; it is modern Xingzhou county; the old city is northwest of the county seat.>
translations.idiomatic: <Bairen in Zhao—northwest of present Longyao in Xingtai prefecture.>

sentences.zh: 漢兵不知而進，前部偏將朱浮、鄧禹為育所破，亡失輜重。

translations.literal: The Han troops did not know and advanced; the van deputy generals Zhu Fu and Deng Yu were defeated by Yu and lost their baggage.
translations.idiomatic: Unaware, the Han vanguard under Zhu Fu and Deng Yu walked into an ambush and lost their wagons,

sentences.zh: 光武在後聞之，收浮、禹散卒，與育戰於郭門，大破之，盡得其所獲。

translations.literal: Guangwu in the rear heard it, gathered Zhu and Deng's scattered soldiers, fought Yu at the outer gate, greatly defeated him, and recovered all that had been taken.
translations.idiomatic: but Guangwu collected the stragglers, met Li Yu at the outer gate of Bairen, routed him, and recaptured every cart.

sentences.zh: 育還保城，攻之不下，於是引兵拔廣阿。

translations.literal: Yu returned to hold the city; he attacked but could not take it; thereupon he led troops and seized Guang'a.
translations.idiomatic: Li Yu shut himself inside; a siege would not crack the walls, so Guangwu swung away and stormed Guang'a,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬鉅鹿郡，故城在今趙州象城縣西北。〉

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Julu commandery; its old city is northwest of Xiangcheng county in Zhao prefecture today.>
translations.idiomatic: <Guang'a in Julu—northwest of the old Xiangcheng county seat.>

sentences.zh: 會上谷太守耿況、漁陽太守彭寵

translations.literal: It happened that the governor of Shanggu, Geng Kuang, and the governor of Yuyang, Peng Chong,
translations.idiomatic: About then Geng Kuang of Shanggu and Peng Chong of Yuyang,

sentences.zh: 〈上谷，郡，故城在今媯州懷戎縣。

translations.literal: <Shanggu commandery; its old city is at modern Huairong county in the Yanqing belt.
translations.idiomatic: <Shanggu's walls stood in the Beijing northwest hills,

sentences.zh: 漁陽，郡，在漁水之陽，今幽州縣。〉

translations.literal: Yuyang commandery lies on the north bank of the Yu River; it is a Youzhou county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: while Yuyang took its name from the 'sunny' north bank of the Yu River.>

sentences.zh: 各遣其將吳漢、寇恂等將突騎來助擊王郎，

translations.literal: each sent their generals Wu Han, Kou Xun, and others leading assault cavalry to help strike Wang Lang,
translations.idiomatic: each dispatched Wu Han, Kou Xun, and other captains with shock cavalry to reinforce the campaign against Wang Lang,

sentences.zh: 〈突騎，言能衝突軍陣。〉

translations.literal: <Assault cavalry means cavalry able to burst enemy formations.'>
translations.idiomatic: <'Shock horse' meant troopers trained to pierce a battle line.>

sentences.zh: 更始亦遣尚書僕射謝躬討郎，

translations.literal: The Gengshi Emperor also sent Vice Director of the Secretariat Xie Gong to punish Lang,
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gengshi ordered Vice Director Xie Gong to operate against Wang Lang,

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「尚書四員，武帝置，成帝加一為五。

translations.literal: <The Han guan yi says: 'The Secretariat had four members, Wudi established them; Chengdi added one to make five.
translations.idiomatic: <The Secretariat began with four bureaus under Emperor Wu; Emperor Cheng added a fifth,

sentences.zh: 有常侍曹尚書，主丞相御史事；

translations.literal: There was the Palace Attendant Secretariat supervisor, in charge of Chancellor and imperial secretary affairs;
translations.idiomatic: among them the palace bureau watched the Chancellor and censor,

sentences.zh: 二千石尚書，主刺史、二千石事；

translations.literal: the two-thousand-shi Secretariat supervisor, in charge of regional inspectors and two-thousand-shi officials;
translations.idiomatic: another tracked inspectors and two-thousand-shi prefects,

sentences.zh: 戶曹尚書，主人庶上書事；

translations.literal: the Household Secretariat supervisor, in charge of commoners' memorials;
translations.idiomatic: the household bureau handled common petitions,

sentences.zh: 主客尚書，主外國四夷事；

translations.literal: the Host-Guest Secretariat supervisor, in charge of foreign tribes;
translations.idiomatic: the host-guest secretariat handled relations with foreign courts and frontier peoples,

sentences.zh: 成帝加三公尚書，主斷獄事。

translations.literal: Chengdi added the Three Dukes Secretariat supervisor, in charge of criminal cases.
translations.idiomatic: and Chengdi added a 'three dukes' desk for appellate justice.

sentences.zh: 僕射，秦官也。

translations.literal: Vice director is a Qin office.
translations.idiomatic: The title vice director (pushe) was Qin in origin,

sentences.zh: 僕，主也。

translations.literal: Pu means supervisor.
translations.idiomatic: pu meaning 'to oversee,'

sentences.zh: 古者重武事，每官必有主射以督課之。

translations.literal: In antiquity they valued military affairs; every office necessarily had a chief archer to supervise training.
translations.idiomatic: because every department once kept a master archer to drill its men.

sentences.zh: 」謝躬為尚書僕射。〉

translations.literal: ' Xie Gong served as vice director of the Secretariat.'>
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong held that vice directorship.>

sentences.zh: 光武因大饗士卒，遂東圍鉅鹿。

translations.literal: Guangwu therefore feasted the soldiers greatly, then east besieged Julu.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu feasted the army and swung east to invest Julu,

sentences.zh: 王郎守將王饒堅守，月餘不下。

translations.literal: Wang Lang's defending general Wang Rao held firm; over a month it did not fall.
translations.idiomatic: where Wang Rao held the walls for more than a month without yielding.

sentences.zh: 郎遣將倪宏、劉奉

translations.literal: Lang sent generals Ni Hong and Liu Feng
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lang then detached Ni Hong and Liu Feng

sentences.zh: 〈倪音五兮反。〉

translations.literal: <Ni is read wu-xi fan.'>
translations.idiomatic: <The surname Ni is read wu-xi.>

sentences.zh: 率數萬人救鉅鹿，光武逆戰於南䜌，

translations.literal: leading several tens of thousands to relieve Julu; Guangwu met them in battle at Nanluan,
translations.idiomatic: with tens of thousands to raise the siege; Guangwu intercepted them at Nanluan,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬鉅鹿郡，故城在今邢州柏人縣東北。

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Julu commandery; its old city is northeast of Bairen county in Xingzhou today.
translations.idiomatic: <Nanluan in Julu—northeast of present Bairen,

sentences.zh: 左傳齊國夏伐晉取欒，即其地也。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition: the Qi minister Guo Xia attacked Jin and took Luan—this is that place.
translations.idiomatic: the spot where the Zuo zhuan places the Jin town of Luan,

sentences.zh: 其後南徙，故加「南」。

translations.literal: Afterwards it moved south, therefore 'south' is added.
translations.idiomatic: later relocated southward—hence the prefix 'South,'

sentences.zh: 今俗謂之倫城，聲之轉也。

translations.literal: Popular speech today calls it Luncheng; it is a sound shift.
translations.idiomatic: folk pronunciation turning the name toward 'Luncheng.'

sentences.zh: 䜌音力全反。〉

translations.literal: Luan is read li-quan fan.'>
translations.idiomatic: The graph in the place name is read li-quan.>

sentences.zh: 斬首數千級。

translations.literal: They beheaded several thousand heads.
translations.idiomatic: The Han charge took several thousand heads.

sentences.zh: 四月，進圍邯鄲，連戰破之。

translations.literal: In the fourth month he advanced to besiege Handan, fighting repeatedly and breaking them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month he closed on Handan and beat sortie after sortie,

sentences.zh: 五月甲辰，拔其城，誅王郎。

translations.literal: On the day jiachen of the fifth month he took the city and executed Wang Lang.
translations.idiomatic: and on jiachen in the fifth month the city fell and Wang Lang died.

sentences.zh: 收文書，得吏人與郎交關謗毀者數千章。

translations.literal: He collected documents and obtained several thousand memorials from clerks and people who had corresponded with Lang and slandered.
translations.idiomatic: His clerks bagged cartloads of letters in which locals had flattered Wang Lang or defamed Guangwu,

sentences.zh: 光武不省，會諸將軍燒之，曰：「令反側子自安。」

translations.literal: Guangwu did not examine them; he assembled the generals and burned them, saying: 'Let the wavering be at peace.'"
translations.idiomatic: but he never opened them—he fed the pile to the flames before his captains, saying, 'Let every man who wavered sleep soundly tonight.'"

sentences.zh: 〈反側，不安也。

translations.literal: <Fan-ce means not at ease.
translations.idiomatic: <Fan-ce means restless minds,

sentences.zh: 詩國風曰：「展轉反側。」〉

translations.literal: The Airs of the States in the Odes says: 'I toss and turn fan-ce.'>
translations.idiomatic: as in the Odes: 'I toss and turn and cannot rest.'>

sentences.zh: 更始遣侍御史持節立光武為蕭王，

translations.literal: The Gengshi Emperor sent a palace attendant censor bearing credentials to enfeoff Guangwu as Prince of Xiao,
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi then sent an attendant censor with the imperial baton to invest him as Prince of Xiao,

sentences.zh: 〈蕭，縣，屬沛郡，今徐州縣也。

translations.literal: <Xiao is a county belonging to Pei commandery; it is a Xuzhou county today.
translations.idiomatic: <Xiao county in Pei—the belt around modern Pei county in northern Jiangsu,

sentences.zh: 《續漢書》曰：「更始使侍御史黃黨封上為蕭王。」〉

translations.literal: The Continued Han History says: 'Gengshi had attendant censor Huang Dang enfeoff him as Prince of Xiao.'>
translations.idiomatic: the Xu Han shu naming Huang Dang as the envoy who bore the patent.>

sentences.zh: 悉令罷兵詣行在所。

translations.literal: He ordered all to dismiss troops and proceed to the traveling headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: The edict ordered him to disband his army and report to the mobile court,

sentences.zh: 〈蔡邕獨斷曰：「天子以四海為家，故謂所居為行在所。」〉

translations.literal: <Cai Yong's Solitary Judgment says: 'The Son of Heaven takes the four seas as his house; therefore what he occupies is called the traveling headquarters.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Cai Yong explains that the emperor's camp is always 'where he happens to be.'>

sentences.zh: 光武辭以河北未平，不就徵。

translations.literal: Guangwu declined on the ground that north of the river was not yet pacified and did not answer the summons.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu pleaded unfinished business in Hebei and stayed put,

sentences.zh: 自是始貳於更始。

translations.literal: From this he began to be of two minds toward the Gengshi Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: and from that moment his loyalty to Gengshi was divided.

sentences.zh: 〈貳，離異也。〉

translations.literal: <Er means to split apart.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Er means estrangement.>

sentences.zh: 是時長安政亂，四方背叛。

translations.literal: At this time Chang'an's government was in chaos and the four quarters rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: While Chang'an slid into anarchy and the provinces rose,

sentences.zh: 梁王劉永擅命睢陽，

translations.literal: Prince of Liang Liu Yong on his own authority issued orders at Suiyang,
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong, king of Liang, dictated law from Suiyang,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬梁郡，今宋州也。

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Liang commandery; it is modern Songzhou.
translations.idiomatic: <Suiyang in Liang—modern Shangqiu on the Henan plain,

sentences.zh: 擅，專也。〉

translations.literal: <Shan means to monopolize.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Shan here means to act unilaterally.>

sentences.zh: 公孫述稱王巴蜀，

translations.literal: Gongshu Shu declared himself king in Ba and Shu,
translations.idiomatic: Gongshu Shu crowned himself in the Ba–Shu basin,

sentences.zh: 〈蜀有巴郡，故緫言之。〉

translations.literal: <Shu had Ba commandery, therefore they are summed together.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Ba and Shu are paired because Ba was a Shu neighbor.>

sentences.zh: 李憲自立為淮南王，

translations.literal: Li Xian set himself up as King of Huainan,
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian styled himself king of Huainan,

sentences.zh: 〈淮南，郡，今壽州也。〉

translations.literal: <Huainan commandery; it is modern Shouzhou.'>
translations.idiomatic: <The old Huainan commandery around Shou county.>

sentences.zh: 秦豐自號楚黎王，

translations.literal: Qin Feng styled himself King Chu of Liqiu,
translations.idiomatic: Qin Feng proclaimed himself 'Chu king of Liqiu,'

sentences.zh: 〈習鑿齒襄陽記曰：「秦豐，黎丘郷人。

translations.literal: <Xi Zuochi's Xiangyang Record says: 'Qin Feng was a man of Liqiu village.
translations.idiomatic: <Xi Zuochi records Qin Feng as a native of Liqiu township,

sentences.zh: 黎丘楚地，故稱楚黎王。

translations.literal: Liqiu is Chu territory; therefore he is called King Chu of Li.
translations.idiomatic: Chu ground—hence the royal style 'Chu of Li,'

sentences.zh: 」黎丘故城在今襄州率道縣北。〉

translations.literal: ' The old city of Liqiu is north of Shuidao county in Xiangzhou today.'>
translations.idiomatic: with its old mound north of present Yicheng in Hubei.>

sentences.zh: 張步起琅邪，

translations.literal: Zhang Bu rose in Langye,
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu carved out Langye,

sentences.zh: 〈郡有琅邪山，故城，今海州朐山縣東北。〉

translations.literal: <The commandery has Mount Langye; its old city is northeast of Qushan county in Haizhou today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Named for Mount Langye near the coast northeast of Lianyungang.>

sentences.zh: 董憲起東海，

translations.literal: Dong Xian rose in Donghai,
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xian seized Donghai,

sentences.zh: 〈郡名，今海州縣。〉

translations.literal: <A commandery name; it is a Haizhou county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <The coastal Donghai belt around Lianyungang.>

sentences.zh: 延岑起漢中，

translations.literal: Yan Cen rose in Hanzhong,
translations.idiomatic: Yan Cen dominated Hanzhong,

sentences.zh: 〈郡名，故城在今梁州南鄭縣東北。〉

translations.literal: <A commandery name; its old city is northeast of Nanzheng county in Liangzhou today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Hanzhong's walls northeast of Hanzhong city in Shaanxi.>

sentences.zh: 田戎起夷陵，

translations.literal: Tian Rong rose at Yiling,
translations.idiomatic: Tian Rong mobilized Yiling,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬南郡。

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Nan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: <Yiling county in Nan commandery,

sentences.zh: 有夷山，故曰夷陵，今硤州縣也，故城在今縣西北。〉

translations.literal: There is Yi Mountain; therefore it is called Yiling; it is a Xiazhou county today; the old city is northwest of the county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: named from Yi Mountain—modern Yichang, the ruins northwest of town.>

sentences.zh: 並置將帥，侵略郡縣。

translations.literal: together they set up generals and invaded commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: Each band installed captains and raided the countryside,

sentences.zh: 又別號諸賊銅馬、大肜、高湖、重連、鐵脛、大搶、尤來、上江、青犢、五校、檀郷、五幡、五樓、富平、獲索等，

translations.literal: also separately titled bandits: Bronze Horse, Great Rong, Gao Lake, Double Link, Iron Shin, Great Spear, Youlai, Upper Yang, Green Calf, Five Camps, Tanxiang, Five Banners, Five Towers, Fuping, Huosuo, and the like,
translations.idiomatic: while nicknamed hosts—the Bronze Horse, Great Rong, Gao Lake, Double Link, Iron Shin, Great Spear, Youlai, Upper River, Green Calf, Five Camps, Tanxiang, Five Banners, Five Towers, Fuping, Huosuo, and more—

sentences.zh: 〈諸賊或以山川土地為名，或以軍容彊盛為號。

translations.literal: <These bandits took their names from mountains, rivers, or lands, or from fearsome military appearance.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Commentators note names drawn from terrain or from bravado.>

sentences.zh: 銅馬賊帥東山荒禿、上淮況等，大肜渠帥樊重，尤來渠帥樊崇，五校賊帥高扈，檀郷賊帥董次仲，五樓賊帥張文，富平賊帥徐少，獲索賊帥古師郎等，並見東觀記。〉

translations.literal: The Bronze Horse chiefs Dongshan Huangtu, Shanghuai Kuang, and others; the Great Rong chief Fan Zhong; the Youlai chief Fan Chong; the Five Camps chief Gao Hu; the Tanxiang chief Dong Cizhong; the Five Towers chief Zhang Wen; the Fuping chief Xu Shao; the Huosuo chief Gu Shilang, and others—all are recorded in the Eastern Lodge Record.'>
translations.idiomatic: The Dongguan ji names the captains of each horde: Huangtu and Kuang for the Bronze Horse, Fan Zhong for the Great Rong, Fan Chong for Youlai, and so on down the list.>

sentences.zh: 各領部曲，

translations.literal: each commanding a following,
translations.idiomatic: each commanding his own column,

sentences.zh: 〈《續漢志》曰：「大將軍營有五部，部三校尉。

translations.literal: <The Continued Han Treatise says: 'The grand general's camp has five divisions; each division has three colonels.
translations.idiomatic: <The Xu Han zhi: a grand general's army had five divisions, three colonels per division,

sentences.zh: 部下有曲，曲有軍候一人。」〉

translations.literal: Below the division are companies; each company has one army commandant.'>
translations.idiomatic: each colonel with companies watched by an army commandant.>

sentences.zh: 衆合數百萬人，所在寇掠。

translations.literal: The hosts together numbered several million men; wherever they were they plundered.
translations.idiomatic: until combined strength ran to millions and pillage was everywhere.

sentences.zh: 光武將擊之，先遣吳漢北發十郡兵。

translations.literal: Guangwu was about to strike them; first he sent Wu Han north to mobilize the troops of ten commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Before engaging the bandit hosts, Guangwu ordered Wu Han north to levy every circuit he could from ten commanderies.

sentences.zh: 幽州牧苗曾不從，漢遂斬曾而發其衆。

translations.literal: Youzhou shepherd Miao Zeng disobeyed; Han thereupon executed Zeng and mobilized his multitude.
translations.idiomatic: Youzhou's shepherd Miao Zeng balked at the levy, so Wu Han beheaded him and drafted his men.

sentences.zh: 秋，光武擊銅馬於鄡，

translations.literal: In autumn Guangwu attacked the Bronze Horse at Gao.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn he brought the Bronze Horse to battle at Gao county,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬鉅鹿郡，故城在今兾州鹿城縣東。

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Julu commandery; its old city is east of Lucheng county in Jizhou today.
translations.idiomatic: <Gao lay in Julu; the ruins stood east of Lucheng in the Jizhou belt,

sentences.zh: 鄡音苦堯反。

translations.literal: Gao is read ku-yao fan.
translations.idiomatic: the place name read ku-yao.

sentences.zh: 竹書紀年曰：「衞鞅封于鄡。

translations.literal: The Bamboo Annals says: 'Wei Yang was enfeoffed at Gao.'
translations.idiomatic: The Zhu shu ji nian records Shang Yang's fief at Gao,

sentences.zh: 」臣賢案：下文云「吳漢將突騎來會清陽」，又「追至館陶」，並與鄡相近。

translations.literal: ' Your servant Xian notes: below it says 'Wu Han led assault cavalry to rendezvous at Qingyang,' and again 'pursued to Guantao'—all close to Gao.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian observes that the narrative later pairs Gao with Qingyang and Guantao on the same march,

sentences.zh: 俗本多誤作「鄔」，而蕭該音一古反，云屬太原郡，臧矜音作鄢，一建反，云屬襄陽郡，並誤也。〉

translations.literal: Popular editions mostly wrongly write another graph; Xiao Gai gives a reading and says it belongs to Taiyuan commandery; Zang Jin reads it as another place in Xiangyang commandery—both are wrong.'>
translations.idiomatic: so miscopying the county graph as homophones in Shanxi or Hubei is simply wrong.>

sentences.zh: 吳漢將突騎來會清陽。

translations.literal: Wu Han led assault cavalry to come join at Qingyang.
translations.idiomatic: while Wu Han swept in with shock cavalry to meet him at Qingyang.

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬清河郡，今貝州縣，故城在州西北。〉

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Qinghe commandery; it is modern Beizhou county; the old city is northwest of the prefectural seat.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Qingyang in Qinghe—northwest of the seat of what was later Beizhou.>

sentences.zh: 賊數挑戰，

translations.literal: The bandits repeatedly challenged to battle.
translations.idiomatic: The rebels baited him day after day,

sentences.zh: 〈挺身獨戰也，古謂之致師，見左傳。

translations.literal: <To bare the body and fight alone; in antiquity it was called 'presenting the army'; see the Zuo Tradition.
translations.idiomatic: <A 'challenge' meant a lone champion stepping out—classical 'presenting troops' in the Zuo zhuan sense,

sentences.zh: 挑音徒了反。〉

translations.literal: Tiao is read tu-liao fan.'>
translations.idiomatic: with the verb read tu-liao.>

sentences.zh: 光武堅營自守；

translations.literal: Guangwu held the camp fast and defended himself;
translations.idiomatic: but Guangwu kept the palisade closed,

sentences.zh: 有出鹵掠者，輒擊取之，

translations.literal: when any went out to raid and plunder, he always struck and took them,
translations.idiomatic: pouncing on every foraging party that strayed out,

sentences.zh: 〈鹵與虜同。

translations.literal: <Lu is the same as the graph for captive.
translations.idiomatic: <Here 'raid' uses the graph usually written for 'captive-taking,'

sentences.zh: 郭璞注爾雅曰：「掠，奪取也。」〉

translations.literal: Guo Pu's commentary on the Erya says: 'Lue means to seize by force.'>
translations.idiomatic: and Guo Pu glosses the verb as outright seizure.>

sentences.zh: 絕其粮道。

translations.literal: He cut off their grain road.
translations.idiomatic: until their supply lines were severed.

sentences.zh: 積月餘日，賊食盡，夜遁去，追至館陶，大破之。

translations.literal: After more than a month the bandits' food was exhausted; they fled by night; he pursued to Guantao and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: After a month their granaries were empty; they broke camp at night and he ran them down at Guantao,

sentences.zh: 〈館陶，縣，屬魏郡，今魏州縣。〉

translations.literal: <Guantao is a county belonging to Wei commandery; it is a Weizhou county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Guantao in Wei—later the seat around Daming in Hebei.>

sentences.zh: 受降未盡，而高湖、重連從東南來，與銅馬餘衆合，光武復與大戰於蒲陽，悉破降之，封其渠帥為列侯。

translations.literal: Before the surrender was fully processed, Gao Lake and Double Link came from the southeast and joined the Bronze Horse remnants; Guangwu again fought a great battle with them at Puyang, completely defeated and accepted their surrender, and enfeoffed their chiefs as full marquises.
translations.idiomatic: while disarmament was still underway, Gao Lake and Double Link swept in from the southeast to weld onto the Bronze Horse survivors; Guangwu met them again at Puyang, broke them utterly, and raised their chiefs to ranked marquisates,

sentences.zh: 〈《前書》音義曰「蒲陽山，蒲水所出」，在今定州北平縣西北。

translations.literal: <The Former Han yin yi says: 'Puyang Mountain is where the Pu River rises,' northwest of Beiping county in Dingzhou today.
translations.idiomatic: <The Han shu yin yi places the battlefield on the Pu watershed northwest of Tang county,

sentences.zh: 本或作「滿陽」。

translations.literal: Some editions write Manyang.'>
translations.idiomatic: though some manuscripts read the name as Manyang.>

sentences.zh: 渠，大也。

translations.literal: Qu means great.
translations.idiomatic: Qu in 'chief' means 'ringleader,'

sentences.zh: 尚書：「殲厥渠魁。

translations.literal: The Documents say: 'Extirpate their great villains.'
translations.idiomatic: as in the Documents: 'wipe out their ringleaders.'

sentences.zh: 」列侯即徹侯也。

translations.literal: Full marquis is thorough marquis.
translations.idiomatic: The rank lie hou is the old che hou,

sentences.zh: 稱列者，言見序列也。〉

translations.literal: Calling them lie means they appear in the ordered ranks.'>
translations.idiomatic: lie simply meaning they were entered on the roster of nobility.>

sentences.zh: 降者猶不自安，光武知其意，勑令各歸營勒兵，乃自乘輕騎按行部陳。

translations.literal: The surrendered still were not at ease in themselves; Guangwu knew their thoughts; he ordered each to return to camp and marshal troops; then he himself rode light cavalry inspecting the battle lines.
translations.idiomatic: The new troops still trembled; Guangwu sensed it and told each band to go back to its own camp and stand to arms—then he toured every formation on a single mount,

sentences.zh: 降者更相語曰：「蕭王推赤心置人腹中，安得不投死乎！」

translations.literal: The surrendered said to one another: 'The Prince of Xiao sets a red heart in men's bellies—how can we not cast ourselves to death!'
translations.idiomatic: until they murmured among themselves, 'The Prince of Xiao lays his bare heart in our stomachs—who would not die for him?'

sentences.zh: 〈投死猶言致死。〉

translations.literal: <Tou si means staking one's life, the same as zhi si ('bringing death upon oneself').>
translations.idiomatic: <The phrase means pledging one's life in loyalty.>

sentences.zh: 由是皆服。

translations.literal: From this they all submitted.
translations.idiomatic: After that display they were his.

sentences.zh: 悉將降人分配諸將，衆遂數十萬，故關西號光武為「銅馬帝」。

translations.literal: He entirely led the surrendered people and distributed them among the generals; the host then numbered several hundred thousand; therefore west of the pass they styled Guangwu the Bronze Horse Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: He parceled the surrendered tens of thousands among his commanders until his army swelled past a hundred thousand—hence the western nickname 'Bronze Horse Emperor.'

sentences.zh: 赤眉別帥與大肜、青犢十餘萬衆在射犬，

translations.literal: A Red Eyebrow deputy chief together with the Great Rong and Green Calf, over a hundred thousand men, were at Shequan,
translations.idiomatic: A Red Eyebrow column still linked with the Great Rong and Green Calf—more than a hundred thousand—held Shequan,

sentences.zh: 〈《續漢志》曰野王縣有射犬聚，故城在今懷州武德縣北也。〉

translations.literal: <The Continued Han Treatise says Yewang county has Shequan settlement; its old city is north of Wude county in Huai prefecture today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <The Xu Han zhi places the hamlet north of what is now Wude in Henan.>

sentences.zh: 光武進擊，大破之，衆皆散走。

translations.literal: Guangwu advanced and attacked, greatly defeated them, and the multitude all scattered and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu marched in, shattered them, and watched the horde scatter,

sentences.zh: 使吳漢、岑彭襲殺謝躬於鄴。

translations.literal: He ordered Wu Han and Cen Peng to raid and kill Xie Gong at Ye.
translations.idiomatic: then had Wu Han and Cen Peng assassinate Xie Gong inside Ye.

sentences.zh: 青犢、赤眉賊入函谷關，攻更始。

translations.literal: The Green Calf and Red Eyebrow bandits entered Hangu Pass and attacked the Gengshi Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: While the Green Calf and Red Eyebrows forced Hangu and marched on Emperor Gengshi,

sentences.zh: 〈函谷，谷名，因谷以名關。

translations.literal: <Hangu is a gorge name; from the gorge the pass takes its name.
translations.idiomatic: <Hangu Pass is named for the narrow gorge,

sentences.zh: 舊在弘農湖城縣西，《前書》楊僕為樓船將軍，有功，恥居關外，武帝乃為徒於新安。

translations.literal: Formerly it was west of Lucheng county in Hongnong; the Former Han History says Yang Pu was tower-ship general, had merit, was ashamed to live outside the pass, and Wudi therefore moved it to Xin'an.
translations.idiomatic: first cut west of Lingbao until Emperor Wu relocated it east to Xin'an so hero officers need not camp 'outside the barrier,'

sentences.zh: 故關在今洛州新安縣之東。〉

translations.literal: Therefore the old pass is east of Xin'an county in Luozhou today.'>
translations.idiomatic: leaving the Ming-era gorge east of Xin'an in western Henan.>

sentences.zh: 光武乃遣鄧禹率六裨將引兵而西，以乘更始、赤眉之亂。

translations.literal: Guangwu thereupon sent Deng Yu leading six deputy generals to lead troops westward, in order to take advantage of the chaos between the Gengshi Emperor and the Red Eyebrows.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu answered by sending Deng Yu with six brigadier generals west to exploit the civil war between Gengshi and the Red Eyebrows,

sentences.zh: 時更始使大司馬朱鮪、舞陰王李軼等屯洛陽，

translations.literal: At that time the Gengshi Emperor had sent Grand Marshal Zhu You, King of Wuyin Li Yi, and others to camp at Luoyang,
translations.idiomatic: while Gengshi's Grand Marshal Zhu You and the king of Wuyin, Li Yi, still held the Luoyang front,

sentences.zh: 〈舞陰，縣，屬南陽郡，故城在今唐州沘陽縣西北。〉

translations.literal: <Wuyin is a county belonging to Nanyang commandery; its old city is northwest of Biyang county in Tangzhou today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Wuyin in Nanyang—northwest of the Biyang district in southern Henan.>

sentences.zh: 光武亦令馮異守孟津以拒之。

translations.literal: Guangwu also ordered Feng Yi to hold Meng Ford to resist them.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu told Feng Yi to seal the Meng crossing and block them,

sentences.zh: 〈孔安國注尚書云：「孟，地名，在洛北，都道所湊，古今以為津。

translations.literal: <Kong Anguo's commentary on the Documents says: 'Meng is a place name north of the Luo; it is where capital roads converge; ancient and modern take it as a ford.
translations.idiomatic: <Kong Anguo glosses Meng as the great ford north of the Luo where every road converged,

sentences.zh: 」論衡曰：「武王伐紂，八百諸侯同於此盟，故曰盟津。

translations.literal: ' The Lun heng says: 'When King Wu punished Zhou, eight hundred lords covenanted here; therefore it is called Covenant Ford.'
translations.idiomatic: and the Lun heng recalls how King Wu's allies swore the covenant there,

sentences.zh: 」俗名治戍津，今河陽縣津也。〉

translations.literal: ' Popular name Garrison Ford; it is the ford of Heyang county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: folk memory still calls it the garrison ford at Heyang.>

sentences.zh: 光武北擊尤來、大搶、五幡於元氏，追至右北平，連破之。

translations.literal: Guangwu struck north at Youlai, the Great Spear, and the Five Banners at Yuanshi, pursued to Right Beiping, and continuously defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: He drove north from Yuanshi against Youlai, the Great Spear, and the Five Banners, chasing them into Zhongshan's Beiping county—later copyists wrongly added 'Right' before Beiping—and beat them again and again,

sentences.zh: 〈北平，縣，屬中山國，今易州永樂縣也。

translations.literal: <Beiping is a county belonging to Zhongshan principality; it is Yongle county in Yizhou today.
translations.idiomatic: <Beiping in Zhongshan corresponds to Yongle on the Yi River plain,

sentences.zh: 臣賢案：東觀記、續漢書並無「右」字，此加「右」，誤也。

translations.literal: Your servant Xian notes: the Eastern Lodge Record and the Continued Han History both lack the graph 'you'; adding 'you' here is an error.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian notes that neither the Dongguan ji nor the Xu Han shu reads 'You Beiping' here—the extra graph is a scribal blunder,

sentences.zh: 營州西南別有右北平郡故城，非此地。〉

translations.literal: Southwest of Yingzhou there is a separate You Beiping commandery old city; it is not this place.'>
translations.idiomatic: because the Liaodong commandery of that name lies far to the northeast.>

sentences.zh: 又戰於順水北，

translations.literal: Again they fought north of the Shun River,
translations.idiomatic: He fought them again on the north bank of the Shun,

sentences.zh: 〈酈元水經注云：「徐水經北平縣故城北，光武追銅馬、五幡，破之於順水，即徐水之別名也。

translations.literal: <Li Daoyuan's Shuijing zhu says: 'The Xu River passes north of Beiping county's old city; Guangwu pursued the Bronze Horse and Five Banners and defeated them at the Shun River—it is another name for the Xu River.'
translations.idiomatic: <Li Daoyuan identifies the stream as the Xu River curling past old Beiping,

sentences.zh: 」在今易州。

translations.literal: ' It is in modern Yizhou.
translations.idiomatic: in the Baoding–Yi county region today,

sentences.zh: 本或作「慎」者，誤也。〉

translations.literal: Editions that write 'Shen' are wrong.'>
translations.idiomatic: and warns against reading the river name as 'Shen.'>

sentences.zh: 乘勝輕進，反為所敗。

translations.literal: Riding victory he pressed lightly forward and in turn was defeated by them.
translations.idiomatic: Flush with victory he pushed too hard and the bandits turned the tables,

sentences.zh: 賊追急，短兵接，

translations.literal: The bandits pressed urgently; short weapons joined.
translations.idiomatic: pursuing so close that swords crossed hilts,

sentences.zh: 〈短兵謂刀劔也。

translations.literal: <Short weapons means swords.
translations.idiomatic: <'Short weapons' means blades,

sentences.zh: 楚辭曰：「車錯轂兮短兵接。」〉

translations.literal: The Chu ci says: 'Chariot hubs cross; short weapons join.'>
translations.idiomatic: as the Chu ci line has it when chariots lock hubs.>

sentences.zh: 光武自投高岸，遇突騎王豐，下馬授光武，光武撫其肩而上，顧笑謂耿弇曰：「幾為虜嗤。

sentences.zh: 十一月甲午，幸懷。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, day jiawu, he visited Huai.
translations.idiomatic: and in the eleventh month returned to Huai on jiawu,

sentences.zh: 劉永自稱天子。

translations.literal: Liu Yong declared himself Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: while Liu Yong proclaimed himself emperor in the east,

sentences.zh: 十二月丙戌，至自懷。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, day bingxu, he arrived from Huai.
translations.idiomatic: returning from Huai on bingxu in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 赤眉殺更始，而隗嚻據隴右，盧芳起安定。

translations.literal: The Red Eyebrows killed the Gengshi Emperor, while Wei Ao held Longyou and Lu Fang rose in Anding.
translations.idiomatic: That season the Red Eyebrows murdered Gengshi, Wei Ao locked the Longxi corridor, and Lu Fang raised Anding,

sentences.zh: 〈郡名，今涇州縣。〉

translations.literal: <A commandery name; it is a Jing prefecture county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Anding commandery around the old Pingliang/Jingzhou belt in Gansu.>

sentences.zh: 破虜大將軍叔壽擊五校賊於曲梁，戰歿。

translations.literal: General Who Destroys Captives Shushou attacked the Five Camps bandits at Quliang and died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: General Who Destroys Captives Shushou died fighting the Five Camps at Quliang,

sentences.zh: 〈曲梁屬廣平國，今洺州縣也。〉

translations.literal: <Quliang belonged to the principality of Guangping; it is a Ming prefecture county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Quliang in Guangping—modern Yongnian in southern Hebei.>

sentences.zh: 二年春正月甲子朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In spring, first month of the second year, on jiazi the first day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of Jianwu 2, new year's day jiazi, the sun was eclipsed,

sentences.zh: 〈《續漢志》曰：「在危八度。

translations.literal: <The Continued Han Treatise says: 'It was at eight degrees of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: <The Xu Han zhi places the disk in the Wei lunar lodge,

sentences.zh: 虛、危，齊地。

translations.literal: Xu and Wei are Qi territory.'
translations.idiomatic: the Xu-Wei sector mapping to Qi,

sentences.zh: 賊張步擁兵據齊，至五年乃破。」〉

translations.literal: The bandit Zhang Bu held troops and occupied Qi; only in the fifth year was he broken.'>
translations.idiomatic: where Zhang Bu still held out until the fifth year of the reign.>

sentences.zh: 大司馬吳漢率九將軍擊檀郷賊於鄴東，大破降之。

translations.literal: Grand Marshal Wu Han led nine generals to strike the Tanxiang bandits east of Ye and greatly defeated and accepted their surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han then took nine generals against Tanxiang east of Ye, broke them, and accepted their surrender,

sentences.zh: 庚辰，封功臣皆為列侯，大國四縣，餘各有差。

translations.literal: On day gengchen he enfeoffed the merit ministers all as full marquises; great states four counties, the rest each according to rank.
translations.idiomatic: and on gengchen he enfeoffed his captains: the greatest fiefs held four counties, the rest graded downward,

sentences.zh: 下詔曰：「人情得足，苦於放縱，快須臾之欲，忘慎罰之義。

translations.literal: He issued an edict saying: 'Human feelings, once satisfied, suffer from indulgence; they relish momentary pleasure and forget the meaning of careful punishment.'
translations.idiomatic: adding an edict: 'Men grow reckless once rewarded; they chase pleasure and forget how carefully punishments must fit crimes,'

sentences.zh: 〈《尚書》曰：「罔不明德慎罰，亦克用勸。

translations.literal: <The Documents says: 'None do not clarify virtue and be careful in punishments; thereby they can use encouragement.'
translations.idiomatic: <The Shang shu urges clear virtue and cautious penalties,

sentences.zh: 」孔安國注云「慎刑罰，亦能用勸善」也。〉

translations.literal: ' Kong Anguo's commentary says: 'Careful in punishments, also able to use encouragement toward good.'>
translations.idiomatic: Kong Anguo glossing that just sentences build good order.>

sentences.zh: 惟諸將業遠功大，誠欲傳於無窮，冝如臨深淵，如履薄冰，戰戰慄慄，日慎一日。

translations.literal: Only you generals have accomplished distant deeds and great merit; if you truly wish to transmit them endlessly, you ought to be as if standing beside a deep abyss, as if treading on thin ice, trembling with fear, daily more careful than the day before.'
translations.idiomatic: yet you who have marched farthest must now guard your fame as if on cliff's edge or thin ice—each day more careful than the last,'

sentences.zh: 〈《太公金匱》曰：「黃帝居人上，惴惴若臨深淵；

translations.literal: <The Taigong metal casket says: 'The Yellow Emperor dwelt above the people, anxious as if beside a deep abyss;
translations.idiomatic: <The Taigong jin kui says the Yellow Emperor ruled in dread,

sentences.zh: 舜居人上，矜矜如履薄冰；

translations.literal: Shun dwelt above the people, cautious as if treading on thin ice;
translations.idiomatic: Shun as if on ice,

sentences.zh: 禹居人上，慄慄如不滿日。

translations.literal: Yu dwelt above the people, trembling as if the sun were not yet full.'
translations.idiomatic: Yu as if noon might never come,'

sentences.zh: 敬勝怠則吉，義勝欲則昌，日慎一日，壽終無殃。」〉

translations.literal: Reverence overcomes negligence then there is auspiciousness; righteousness overcomes desire then there is flourishing; daily more careful than the day before, life ends without calamity.'>
translations.idiomatic: promising blessing to the reverent and ruin to the rash.>

sentences.zh: 其顯效未詶，名籍未立者，大鴻臚趣上，

translations.literal: Those whose conspicuous merit is not yet requited and whose name registers are not yet established—the grand herald shall hasten to report upward,
translations.idiomatic: Any hero whose reward still lags or whose patent is not filed—the grand herald must hurry the paperwork,

sentences.zh: 〈《續漢志》曰：「大鴻臚，卿一人，中二千石，掌諸王入朝及拜諸侯封者。

translations.literal: <The Continued Han Treatise says: 'The grand herald, one minister, salary equivalent to two thousand shi, supervises kings of principalities coming to court and investing marquises who are enfeoffed.'
translations.idiomatic: <The Xu Han zhi makes the grand herald keeper of enfeoffment lists,

sentences.zh: 」趣音促。〉

translations.literal: ' Qu is read cu.'>
translations.idiomatic: qu here read cu, 'hasten.'>

sentences.zh: 朕將差而錄之。

translations.literal: We shall differentiate and record them.'"
translations.idiomatic: and the throne will sort and confirm each grant.'"

sentences.zh: 」博士丁恭議曰：「古帝王封諸侯不過百里，

translations.literal: ' Erudite Ding Gong debated, saying: 'Of old emperors and kings enfeoffed the lords of principalities not exceeding a hundred li,'
translations.idiomatic: Erudite Ding Gong objected: 'Ancient kings never gave a vassal more than a hundred li,'

sentences.zh: 〈《史記》太史公曰：「武王、成、康所封數百，而同姓五十，地不過百里。」〉

translations.literal: <The Shi ji, Grand Scribe's remark, says: 'Those whom King Wu, Cheng, and Kang enfeoffed numbered in the hundreds; of the same surname fifty; their lands did not exceed a hundred li.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Sima Qian recalls that even Zhou enfeoffments rarely passed a hundred li,

sentences.zh: 故利以建侯，取法於雷，

translations.literal: Therefore it is favorable to establish lords; one takes model from thunder,
translations.idiomatic: hence the Changes line 'favorable to establish lords' paired with thunder's hundred-li roll,

sentences.zh: 〈易屯卦震下坎上，震為雷，初九曰「利建侯」，又曰「震驚百里」，故封諸侯地方百里，以法雷也。〉

translations.literal: <The Changes Tun hexagram is thunder below water above; thunder is thunder; the first nine says 'favorable to establish lords,' and also says 'thunder shocks a hundred li'; therefore enfeoffing the lords of principalities land of a hundred li square takes thunder as model.'>
translations.idiomatic: <The commentary ties the hundred-li fief to the shock of thunder in the Tun hexagram.>

sentences.zh: 強榦弱枝，所以為治也。

translations.literal: Strengthening the trunk and weakening the branches is the means of good order.'
translations.idiomatic: strong center and weak limbs being the recipe for stability,'

sentences.zh: 今封諸侯四縣，不合法制。

translations.literal: Now enfeoffing the lords of principalities with four counties does not accord with legal institutions.'
translations.idiomatic: so four counties per marquis breaks precedent,'

sentences.zh: 」帝曰：「古之亡國，皆以無道，未甞聞功臣地多而滅亡者。

translations.literal: ' The emperor said: 'States of old perished all through lack of the Way; I have never heard of a merit minister perishing because his land was large.'
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu answered, 'Ancient realms fell through vice, not through rewarding captains too richly,'

sentences.zh: 」乃遣謁者即授印綬，

translations.literal: ' Thereupon he sent heralds immediately to grant seals and ribbons,
translations.idiomatic: and sent heralds the same day to deliver seals and ribbons,

sentences.zh: 〈《前書》曰：「謁者，秦官，掌賔讚受事，員七十人，秩比六百石。

translations.literal: <The Former Han History says: 'Heralds were a Qin office, in charge of guest salutation and receiving business, seventy members, salary equivalent to six hundred shi.'
translations.idiomatic: <Han shu: heralds were Qin ushers at six hundred shi,

sentences.zh: 」中興但三十人。

translations.literal: ' At restoration there were only thirty.'
translations.idiomatic: thirty remaining at the restoration,

sentences.zh: 蔡質《典職儀》曰：「皆選儀容端正，任奉使者。

translations.literal: Cai Zhi's Dian zhi yi says: 'All are chosen for upright bearing and correct demeanor, appointed to serve as envoys.'
translations.idiomatic: Cai Zhi adding that only men of impeccable bearing carried patents,

sentences.zh: 」《前書》曰：「諸侯王，金璽盭綬。

translations.literal: ' The Former Han History says: 'Kings of principalities, gold seal with green-tassel ribbon.'
translations.idiomatic: while kings took gold seals with green tassels,

sentences.zh: 列侯，金印紫綬。

translations.literal: Full marquises, gold seal with purple ribbon.'
translations.idiomatic: full marquises gold with purple,

sentences.zh: 」盭音戾，草名也。

translations.literal: ' Li is read li; it is a grass name.
translations.idiomatic: the royal green 'li' tassel named after a dye plant,

sentences.zh: 似艾，可染綠，因以名綬也。〉

translations.literal: Like mugwort, it can dye green; therefore it names the ribbon.'>
translations.idiomatic: like wormwood used for green dye.>

sentences.zh: 策曰：「在上不驕，高而不危；

translations.literal: The policy document said: 'Above, not arrogant; high yet not perilous;
translations.idiomatic: The patent read: 'High station without arrogance,

sentences.zh: 制節謹度，滿而不溢。

translations.literal: regulate measure and be careful in degree; full yet not overflowing.
translations.idiomatic: measure and restraint so fullness never spills,

sentences.zh: 敬之戒之。

translations.literal: Revere this and be on guard.'
translations.idiomatic: revere these warnings,'

sentences.zh: 傳爾子孫，長為漢藩。」

translations.literal: Transmit to your sons and grandsons; long be a fence for Han.'"
translations.idiomatic: and hand the fief down as a shield for the Liu house.'"

sentences.zh: 〈藩，屏也。

translations.literal: <Fan means screen.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Fan means bulwark,

sentences.zh: 言建諸侯所以為國之藩蔽也。

translations.literal: It means establishing the lords of principalities to be the state's screens and covers.'
translations.idiomatic: the sense being that vassals screen the throne.'

sentences.zh: 詩大雅曰：「四國于藩。」〉

translations.literal: The Greater Odes of the Odes says: 'The four states are your fences.'>
translations.idiomatic: as the Shi jing says of the four quarters as fence.>

sentences.zh: 壬午，更始復漢將軍鄧曅、輔漢將軍于匡降，皆復爵位。

translations.literal: On day renwu the Gengshi generals Restore Han Deng Ye and Assist Han Yu Kuang surrendered; both were restored to noble rank.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu Gengshi holdouts Deng Ye and Yu Kuang submitted and recovered their titles,

sentences.zh: 壬子，起高廟，建社稷於洛陽，立郊兆于城南，始正火德，色尚赤。

translations.literal: On day renzi he raised Gaozu's temple, established the altars of soil and grain at Luoyang, set suburban offering precincts south of the city, and for the first time corrected the fire virtue, with color esteeming red.
translations.idiomatic: on renzi he dedicated the High Ancestor's shrine, planted the soil-and-millet altars at Luoyang, laid out the suburban altar south of the walls, and formally embraced Han's fire phase with red as the court color,

sentences.zh: 〈《漢禮制度》曰：「人君之居，前有朝，後有寢。

translations.literal: <The Han li zhi du says: 'The ruler's dwelling has court in front and chamber behind.'
translations.idiomatic: <Court ritual paired front court with rear chamber,

sentences.zh: 終則制廟以象朝，後制寢以象寢。

translations.literal: At the end he then made the temple to imitate the court, and made the chamber behind to imitate the chamber.
translations.idiomatic: then mirrored them in temple and private shrine,

sentences.zh: 光武都洛陽，乃合高祖以下至平帝為一廟，藏十一帝主於其中。

translations.literal: Guangwu having capital at Luoyang, then combined Gaozu down to Emperor Ping into one temple, storing eleven emperors' spirit tablets therein.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu housing eleven sovereign tablets from Gaozu through Ping in one Luoyang temple,

sentences.zh: 元帝次當第八，光武第九，故立元帝為祖廟，後遵而不改。

translations.literal: Yuandi was next to be eighth; Guangwu ninth; therefore he established Yuandi as the ancestor temple, afterwards following and not changing.'
translations.idiomatic: set Yuan as nominal 'ancestor' because the count made Guangwu ninth in line, a precedent later courts kept,

sentences.zh: 」《續漢志》曰：「立社稷於洛陽，在宗廟之右，皆方壇，四面及中各依方色，無屋，有牆門而已。

translations.literal: ' The Continued Han Treatise says: 'He established soil and grain at Luoyang, to the right of the clan temple; both were square mounds; each face and the center followed the color of its direction; there were no buildings, only a wall and gate.'
translations.idiomatic: the Xu Han zhi describing square earthen altars east of the imperial shrines,

sentences.zh: 」白虎通曰：「天子之壇方五丈，諸侯之壇半天子之壇。

translations.literal: ' The Baihu tong says: 'The Son of Heaven's mound is five zhang square; the lords of principalities' mounds are half the Son of Heaven's mound.'
translations.idiomatic: while the Baihu tong fixes the sovereign's altar at five zhang and vassal altars at half that span.'

sentences.zh: 社者，土也，人非土不立，非穀不食，故封土立社，示有土也。

translations.literal: The soil god is earth; man without earth does not stand; without grain he does not eat; therefore they enclose earth and establish the soil god, showing that there is land.
translations.idiomatic: The grain altar means earth: without land men cannot live, without grain they cannot eat—hence the earthen mound of the soil altar,

sentences.zh: 稷者，五穀之長，得陰陽中和之氣，故祭之也。

translations.literal: The grain lord is the chief of the five grains; it obtains the balanced qi of yin and yang; therefore they sacrifice to it.'
translations.idiomatic: while the millet lord is chief of the five grains, embodying balanced qi—both receive sacrifice.'

sentences.zh: 」《續漢書》曰：「制郊兆於洛陽城南七里，為壇，八陛，中又為重壇，天地位皆在壇上。

translations.literal: ' The Continued Han History says: 'They regulated the suburban precinct south of Luoyang at seven li, making a mound with eight stairways; inside they made a double mound; positions of Heaven and Earth were both on the mound.'
translations.idiomatic: The Xu Han shu lays out the southern suburb: seven li south of Luoyang, an eight-ramp terrace with an inner double tier for Heaven and Earth,

sentences.zh: 其外壇上為五帝位，青帝位在甲寅，赤帝位在丙巳，黃帝位在丁未，白帝位在庚申，黑帝位在壬亥。

translations.literal: On the outer mound were set the positions of the Five Thearchs: the Green Thearch's position at jiayin, the Red Thearch at bingsi, the Yellow Thearch at dingwei, the White Thearch at gengshen, the Black Thearch at renhai.
translations.idiomatic: the outer ring holding the five directional sovereigns in their stem-branch slots,

sentences.zh: 其外為壝，重營皆紫，以象紫宮。

translations.literal: Outside that was the ritual wall; double enclosures were all purple, to image the Purple Palace.
translations.idiomatic: purple double palisades symbolizing the circumpolar court,

sentences.zh: 營有通道以為門，日月在營內南道，日在東，月在西。

translations.literal: The enclosures had passageways serving as gates; sun and moon were on the south path inside the enclosure, the sun in the east, the moon in the west.
translations.idiomatic: with gated lanes: sun east and moon west along the southern track,

sentences.zh: 北斗在北道之西。

translations.literal: The Northern Dipper was west of the north path.
translations.idiomatic: the Big Dipper west of the northern aisle,

sentences.zh: 外營、中營凡千五百一十四神，高皇帝配食焉。

translations.literal: Outer and inner enclosures together had one thousand five hundred fourteen spirits; Gaozu received joint offering there.
translations.idiomatic: fifteen hundred fourteen spirits in all sharing the rite, Gaozu paired as chief ancestor,

sentences.zh: 北郊在洛陽城北四里，方壇，四陛。

translations.literal: The northern suburb was four li north of Luoyang, a square mound with four stairways.
translations.idiomatic: while the northern suburb sat four li north—a square terrace with four ramps,

sentences.zh: 地祇位南面，西上；

translations.literal: The Earth spirit's position faced south, west ascending;
translations.idiomatic: Earth facing south with seats mounting from the west,

sentences.zh: 高皇后配，西面，皆在壇上；

translations.literal: Empress Gao received joint offering, facing west; all were on the mound;
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lü paired to the west on the upper terrace,

sentences.zh: 地理羣后從食，皆在壇下；

translations.literal: the terrestrial host of lords received secondary offerings, all below the mound;
translations.idiomatic: lesser earth gods ranked below,

sentences.zh: 中岳在末；

translations.literal: the central marchmount came last;
translations.idiomatic: central Marchmount closing the row,

sentences.zh: 四岳各依其方，淮、海俱在東，河在西，濟在北，江在南，餘山川各如其方。

translations.literal: the four marchmounts each according to its direction; Huai and sea both in the east, the River in the west, Ji in the north, Yangtze in the south; the remaining mountains and streams each according to their direction.'
translations.idiomatic: the four peaks by quarter, the great rivers in their proper sectors.'

sentences.zh: 」漢初土德，色尚黃，至此始明火德，妃幟尚赤，服色於是乃正。〉

translations.literal: ' At Han's beginning it was earth virtue, color esteeming yellow; only here was fire virtue clarified, consorts' banners esteemed red, and court dress colors were thereupon corrected.'>
translations.idiomatic: Early Han had claimed earth and yellow; Guangwu now proclaimed fire and red as orthodox court colors.>

sentences.zh: 是月，赤眉焚西京宮室，發掘園陵，

translations.literal: In this month the Red Eyebrows burned the Western Capital's palaces and halls and opened the park tombs,
translations.idiomatic: That month the Red Eyebrows torched Chang'an's palaces and looted the imperial tombs,

sentences.zh: 〈園謂塋域，陵謂山墳。〉

translations.literal: <Park means burial ground; tomb means hill grave.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Yuan is the mausoleum park, ling the burial mound.>

sentences.zh: 寇掠關中。

translations.literal: they raided the Guanzhong.
translations.idiomatic: then swept the Guanzhong plain for plunder,

sentences.zh: 大司徒鄧禹入長安，遣府掾奉十一帝神主，納於高廟。

translations.literal: Grand Steward Deng Yu entered Chang'an and sent a steward clerk to convey the eleven emperors' spirit tablets and deposit them in Gaozu's temple.
translations.idiomatic: until Deng Yu rode into Chang'an and had eleven imperial tablets escorted to the High Ancestor's shrine in Luoyang,

sentences.zh: 〈《漢官儀》曰：「司徒府掾屬三十一人，秩千石。

translations.literal: <The Han guan yi says: 'The grand steward's office has thirty-one clerical subordinates at one thousand shi.'
translations.idiomatic: <The Han guan yi lists thirty-one steward's clerks at a thousand shi,

sentences.zh: 」十一帝謂高祖至平帝。

translations.literal: ' The eleven emperors means Gaozu down to Emperor Ping.'
translations.idiomatic: meaning sovereigns from Gaozu through Ping,

sentences.zh: 神主，以木為之，方尺二寸，穿中央，達四方。

translations.literal: Spirit tablets are made of wood, one chi two cun square, bored through the center, communicating to the four quarters.
translations.idiomatic: each tablet a foot-two block of wood pierced to 'open' to the four directions,

sentences.zh: 天子主長尺二寸，諸侯主長一尺，虞主用桑。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven's tablet is one chi two cun long; lords' tablets one foot long; yu tablets use mulberry.
translations.idiomatic: the emperor's tablet longest, vassals' shorter, interim mulberry tablets before burial,

sentences.zh: 練主用栗。

translations.literal: Lian tablets use chestnut.'
translations.idiomatic: permanent chestnut after the lien rite.'

sentences.zh: 衞宏舊漢儀曰：「已葬，收主，為木函，藏廟太室中西壁坎中，去地六尺一寸，祭則立主於坎下。」〉

translations.literal: Wei Hong's Old Han Ceremonial says: 'After burial they gather the tablet, make a wooden casket, store it in the great chamber's west-wall niche in the temple, six chi one cun from the ground; at sacrifice they set the tablet below the niche.'>
translations.idiomatic: Wei Hong adds how the tablets were boxed and lodged in the temple wall until offerings called them forth.>

sentences.zh: 真定王楊、臨邑侯讓謀反，

translations.literal: King Yang of Zhending and Marquis of Linyi Rang plotted rebellion,
translations.idiomatic: King Yang of Zhending and his brother Rang, marquis of Linyi, plotted revolt,

sentences.zh: 〈楊，景帝七代孫。

translations.literal: <Yang was the seventh-generation descendant of Jingdi.'
translations.idiomatic: <Yang was Emperor Jing's seventh-generation descendant,

sentences.zh: 讓即楊弟。〉

translations.literal: Rang is Yang's younger brother.'>
translations.idiomatic: Rang being his younger brother.>

sentences.zh: 遣前將軍耿純誅之。

translations.literal: He sent former general Geng Chun to execute them.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu sent Geng Chun to extirpate them,

sentences.zh: 二月己酉，幸修武。

translations.literal: Second month, day jiyou, he visited Xiuwu.
translations.idiomatic: and on jiyou in the second month visited Xiuchu,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬河內郡，本殷之甯邑。

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Henei commandery; originally the Yin capital Ningyi.'
translations.idiomatic: <Xiuchu in Henei—old Shang territory once called Ning,

sentences.zh: 韓詩外傳曰：「武王伐紂，勒兵於甯，改曰修武。

translations.literal: Han shi wai zhuan says: 'When King Wu attacked Zhou he drilled troops at Ning and changed the name to Xiuchu.'
translations.idiomatic: renamed Xiuchu when King Wu paused there to drill,

sentences.zh: 」今懷州縣也。〉

translations.idiomatic: on jiachen he drew up six hosts: Wu Han's veterans in the van, the main body behind, shock horse and palace guard on the wings,

sentences.zh: 赤眉望見震怖，遣使乞降。

translations.literal: The Red Eyebrows saw and were shaken with terror; they sent envoys begging to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: until the Red Eyebrows quailed and sued for peace,

sentences.zh: 丙午，赤眉君臣面縛，

translations.literal: On day bingwu the Red Eyebrow lord and ministers bound their hands behind their backs,
translations.idiomatic: on bingwu their chiefs came hands bound behind backs,

sentences.zh: 〈面，偝也。

translations.literal: <Mian means back.'
translations.idiomatic: <Mian means the back turned,

sentences.zh: 謂反偝而縛之。〉

translations.literal: It means they turned their backs and were bound.'>
translations.idiomatic: the phrase describing arms tied behind.>

sentences.zh: 奉高皇帝璽綬，

translations.literal: and presented Gaozu's seal and ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: bearing Gaozu's imperial seal and cords,

sentences.zh: 〈蔡邕《獨斷》曰：「皇帝六璽，皆玉螭虎紐，文曰『皇帝行璽』、『皇帝之璽』、『皇帝信璽』、『天子行璽』、『天子之璽』、『天子信璽』，皆以武都紫泥封之。

translations.literal: <Cai Yong's Solitary Judgment says: 'The emperor has six seals, all with jade and tiger knobs, bearing the phrases for imperial progress, imperial authority, imperial trust, celestial progress, celestial authority, and celestial trust; all sealed with Wudu purple mud.'
translations.idiomatic: <Cai Yong lists the six imperial jade seals with their paired 'emperor' and 'Son of Heaven' legends, sealed in purple paste,

sentences.zh: 」《玉璽譜》曰：「傳國璽是秦始皇初定天下所刻，其玉出藍田山，丞相李斯所書，其文曰『受命于天，旣壽永昌』。

translations.literal: ' The Jade Seal Register says: 'The transmission seal was first carved when the First Emperor settled All Under Heaven; the jade came from Mount Lantian; Chancellor Li Si wrote the text: Mandated by Heaven, long life and eternal glory.'
translations.idiomatic: while the fabled 'transmission' jade bore Li Si's eight characters of mandate,

sentences.zh: 高祖至霸上，秦王子嬰獻之。

translations.literal: When Gaozu reached Bashang, Qin prince Ziying presented it.
translations.idiomatic: passed from Ziying to Gaozu at Bashang,
