translations.literal: When Wang Mang usurped the throne he sought the seal from Empress Yuan and she would not give; he threatened with force; then she brought out the seal and threw it on the ground.
translations.idiomatic: seized by Wang Mang from the empress dowager who dashed it down—chipping one horn,

sentences.zh: 璽上螭一角缺。

translations.literal: On the seal the horned tiger was missing one horn.
translations.idiomatic: the chipped horn still visible,

sentences.zh: 及莽敗，李松持璽詣宛上更始；

translations.literal: When Mang fell, Li Song took the seal to Wan and presented it to the Gengshi Emperor;
translations.idiomatic: then carried by Li Song to Gengshi at Wan,

sentences.zh: 更始敗，璽入赤眉；

translations.literal: when the Gengshi Emperor fell, the seal entered the Red Eyebrows;
translations.idiomatic: passed to the Red Eyebrows with Liu Penzi,

sentences.zh: 劉盆子旣敗，以奉光武。」〉

translations.literal: when Liu Penzi was defeated, he presented it to Guangwu.'>
translations.idiomatic: until Penzi's surrender brought it to Guangwu.>

sentences.zh: 詔以屬城門校尉。

translations.literal: An edict ordered it turned over to the colonel of the city gates.
translations.idiomatic: An edict lodged the regalia with the colonel of the city gates,

sentences.zh: 〈《前書》曰「城門校尉，掌京師城門屯兵，秩比二千石」也。〉

translations.literal: <The Former Han History says: 'The colonel of the city gates commands the capital gates' garrison, salary equivalent to two thousand shi.'>
translations.idiomatic: <That officer commanded the Luoyang gate guard at two thousand shi.>

sentences.zh: 戊申，至自冝陽，己酉，詔曰：「羣盜縱橫，賊害元元，盆子竊尊號，亂惑天下。

translations.literal: On day wushen he arrived from Yiyang; on day jiyou an edict said: 'Bandit hosts run rampant, injuring the black-haired people; Penzi stole a supreme title and threw All Under Heaven into confusion.'
translations.idiomatic: On wushen he returned from Yiyang; next day he proclaimed that Penzi's puppet throne had tormented the people,

sentences.zh: 朕奮兵討擊，應時崩解，十餘萬衆束手降服，先帝璽綬歸之王府。

translations.literal: We raised troops to strike and punish; they collapsed in season; more than a hundred thousand submitted with bound hands; the Former Emperor's seal and ribbon returned to the royal storehouse.
translations.idiomatic: the campaign broke them in short order, disarmed a hundred thousand men, and brought Gaozu's seal back to the Han treasury,

sentences.zh: 斯皆祖宗之靈，士人之力，朕曷足以享斯哉！

translations.literal: This is all the spirits of the ancestors and the strength of gentlemen; how could We alone deserve to enjoy this!'
translations.idiomatic: yet that victory was the work of the imperial ancestors and every loyal officer—how could the throne claim it alone?'

sentences.zh: 〈享，當也。〉

translations.literal: <Xiang means to match.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Xiang here means 'to deserve.'>

sentences.zh: 其擇吉日祠高廟，賜天下長子當為父後者爵，人一級。」

translations.literal: Let them choose an auspicious day to sacrifice at Gaozu's temple and grant the empire's eldest sons who shall succeed fathers one rank of nobility each.'"
translations.idiomatic: He ordered a thanksgiving at Gaozu's shrine and a one-rank gift to every eldest son who would inherit a household.'"

sentences.zh: 二月己未，祠高廟，受傳國璽。

translations.literal: Second month, day jiwei, he sacrificed at Gaozu's temple and received the transmission seal.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the second month he took the transmission seal before Gaozu's tablet,

sentences.zh: 劉永立董憲為海西王，

translations.literal: Liu Yong established Dong Xian as king of Haixi,
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong named Dong Xian king of Haixi,

sentences.zh: 〈海西，縣，屬琅邪郡。〉

translations.literal: <Haixi is a county belonging to Langye commandery.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Haixi county in coastal Shandong.>

sentences.zh: 張步為齊王。

translations.literal: Zhang Bu as king of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: and Zhang Bu king of Qi,

sentences.zh: 步殺光祿大夫伏隆而反。

translations.literal: Bu killed the grand master of splendid brightness Fu Long and rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: until Zhang Bu murdered Fu Long and revolted,

sentences.zh: 幸懷。

translations.literal: He visited Huai.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu traveled to Huai,

sentences.zh: 遣吳漢率二將軍擊青犢於軹西，大破降之。

translations.literal: He sent Wu Han leading two generals to strike the Green Calf west of Zhi and greatly defeated and accepted their surrender.
translations.idiomatic: sent Wu Han west of Zhi against the Green Calf, broke them, and took their surrender,

sentences.zh: 〈軹，縣，屬河內郡，故城在今洛州濟源縣東南。〉

translations.literal: <Zhi is a county belonging to Henei commandery; its old city is southeast of Jiyuan county in Luozhou today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Zhi southeast of Jiyuan, Henan.>

sentences.zh: 三月壬寅，以大司徒司直伏湛為大司徒。

translations.literal: Third month, day renyin, he made the grand steward's investigator Fu Zhan grand steward.
translations.idiomatic: on renyin he promoted Fu Zhan from steward's investigator to grand steward,

sentences.zh: 〈《續漢志》曰：「光武即位，依武帝故事置司徒司直，建武十一年省。」〉

translations.literal: <The Continued Han Treatise says: 'When Guangwu assumed the throne he followed Emperor Wu's precedent in setting a steward's investigator; in Jianwu 11 it was abolished.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Guangwu briefly revived the steward's investigator post from Emperor Wu's day.>

sentences.zh: 彭寵陷薊城，寵自立為燕王。

translations.literal: Peng Chong captured Ji city; Chong set himself up as king of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong seized Ji and declared himself king of Yan,

sentences.zh: 帝自將征鄧奉，幸堵陽。

translations.literal: The emperor personally led a campaign against Deng Feng and visited Duyang.
translations.idiomatic: while Guangwu marched on Deng Feng, halting at Duyang,

sentences.zh: 夏四月，大破鄧奉於小長安，斬之。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, he greatly defeated Deng Feng at Little Chang'an and executed him.
translations.idiomatic: and in the fourth month shattered Deng Feng at Little Chang'an and beheaded him,

sentences.zh: 馮異與延岑戰於上林，破之。

translations.literal: Feng Yi fought Yan Cen at Shanglin and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi meanwhile drove Yan Cen from the Shanglin park,

sentences.zh: 〈關中上林苑也。〉

translations.literal: <Guanzhong's Shanglin park.'>
translations.idiomatic: <The old imperial hunting park near Chang'an.>

sentences.zh: 吳漢率七將軍與劉永將蘇茂戰於廣樂，大破之。

translations.literal: Wu Han led seven generals to fight Liu Yong's general Su Mao at Guangle and greatly defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han met Su Mao at Guangle and broke him,

sentences.zh: 〈廣樂地闕，今宋州虞城縣有長樂故城，蓋避隋煬帝諱。〉

translations.literal: <Guangle's location is missing; today Yucheng county in Songzhou has the old city of Changle, probably avoiding taboo on the Sui emperor's name.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Toponym uncertain—likely near Yucheng, Henan, renamed to avoid Sui taboo.>

sentences.zh: 虎牙大將軍蓋延圍劉永於睢陽。

translations.literal: General-in-Chief Who Bares Tiger Teeth Gai Yan besieged Liu Yong at Suiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Yan kept Liu Yong bottled in Suiyang,

sentences.zh: 五月己酉，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: Fifth month, day jiyou, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: and on jiyou in the fifth month Guangwu re-entered the palace,

sentences.zh: 乙卯晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On yimao, the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: while the month's last day yimao brought an eclipse,

sentences.zh: 〈《續漢志》曰：「日在柳十四度。

translations.literal: <The Continued Han Treatise says: 'The sun was at fourteen degrees of Liu.'
translations.idiomatic: <The Xu Han zhi places the sun in the Liu lodge,

sentences.zh: 柳，河南也。

translations.literal: Liu is Henan territory.'
translations.idiomatic: the Willow lunar lodge mapping to the Henan sky quarter,

sentences.zh: 時樊崇謀作亂，其七月伏誅。」〉

translations.literal: At that time Fan Chong plotted rebellion; in the seventh month he was executed in secret.'>
translations.idiomatic: omen tied to Fan Chong's later conspiracy and death.>

sentences.zh: 六月壬戌，大赦天下。

translations.literal: Sixth month, day renxu, he greatly amnestied All Under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On renxu in the sixth month he amnestied again,

sentences.zh: 耿弇與延岑戰於穰，大破之。

translations.literal: Geng Yan fought Yan Cen at Rang and greatly defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan crushed Yan Cen at Rang,

sentences.zh: 〈穰，縣，屬南陽郡，今鄧州縣。〉

translations.literal: <Rang is a county belonging to Nanyang commandery; it is a Dengzhou county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Rang in Nanyang—modern Dengzhou area.>

sentences.zh: 秋七月，征南大將軍岑彭率三將軍伐秦豐，戰於黎丘，大破之，獲其將蔡宏。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, southern campaign general Cen Peng led three generals to campaign against Qin Feng, fought at Liqiu, greatly defeated him, and captured his general Cai Hong.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, in the seventh month, Cen Peng struck Qin Feng at Liqiu, broke his line, and captured Cai Hong,

translations.idiomatic: On gengchen he ruled that any county magistrate under six hundred shi must obtain imperial leave before arrest,

translations.literal: <The Continued Han Treatise says: 'Large counties set one magistrate at one thousand shi;
translations.idiomatic: <Large counties had thousand-shi magistrates,

translations.literal: next set a chief at four hundred shi;
translations.idiomatic: midsize chiefs at four hundred,

translations.literal: small ones three hundred shi.'
translations.idiomatic: small ones three hundred,

translations.literal: Marquis-states' chiefs likewise.'
translations.idiomatic: with the same ladder for marquisate seats,

sentences.zh: 皆掌理人，並秦制。」〉

translations.literal: All govern the people; together they are Qin institutions.'>
translations.idiomatic: all Qin-era survivals.>

translations.literal: Men over eighty or under ten and women implicated in joint crimes—unless it is lack of the Way or those named in arrest edicts—may not be bound.
translations.idiomatic: He exempted octogenarians, infants, and implicated women from jail except in capital crimes or named warrants,

sentences.zh: 〈詔書有名而特捕者。〉

translations.literal: <Named in edicts for special arrest.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Unless the edict singled them out.>

sentences.zh: 當驗問者即就驗。

translations.literal: Those who should be interrogated shall be interrogated on the spot.
translations.idiomatic: and ordered quick inquest for those who still needed questioning,

sentences.zh: 女徒雇山歸家。」

translations.literal: Female convicts may ransom mountain labor and return home.'"
translations.idiomatic: while female laborers could buy out their 'mountain service' and go home.'"

translations.idiomatic: <Han statute let women commute gulag to a monthly lumber fee.>

sentences.zh: 蓋延拔睢陽，獲劉永，而蘇茂、周建立永子紆為梁王。

translations.literal: Gai Yan took Suiyang, captured Liu Yong, but Su Mao and Zhou Jian set up Yong's son Yu as king of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Yan finally stormed Suiyang and killed Yong, only for Su Mao and Zhou Jian to set Yong's son Yu on the Liang throne as puppet,

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬申，幸舂陵，祠園廟，因置酒舊宅，大會故人父老。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, day renshen, he visited Chunling, sacrificed at the park temple, thereupon set out wine at the old residence, and greatly feasted old friends and village elders.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen in the tenth month he toured Chunling, offered at the family tombs, feasted his boyhood neighbors at the old farm,

sentences.zh: 〈光武舊宅在今隨州棗陽縣東南。

translations.literal: <Guangwu's old residence is southeast of Zaoyang county in Suizhou today.'
translations.idiomatic: <The farm stood southeast of Zaoyang in Hubei,

sentences.zh: 宅南二里有白水焉，即張衡所謂「龍飛白水」也。〉

translations.literal: Two li south of the residence is the White Water—what Zhang Heng called the dragon flying on white water.'>
translations.idiomatic: two li from the White Water ford Zhang Heng sang of.>

sentences.zh: 十一月乙未，至自舂陵。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, day yiwei, he arrived from Chunling.
translations.idiomatic: He rode back to Luoyang on yiwei in the eleventh month,

sentences.zh: 涿郡太守張豐反。

translations.literal: The grand administrator of Zhuo commandery, Zhang Feng, rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: while Zhang Feng, prefect of Zhuo, rose in revolt,

sentences.zh: 〈涿郡故城在今幽州范陽縣。〉

translations.literal: <Zhuo commandery's old city is at Fanyang county in Youzhou today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Zhuo's seat at modern Zhuozhou.>

sentences.zh: 是歲，李憲自稱天子。

translations.literal: In this year Li Xian declared himself Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: and Li Xian declared himself emperor in the south,

sentences.zh: 西州大將軍隗嚻奉奏。

translations.literal: Western provinces grand general Wei Ao presented a memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Ao, grand general of the western provinces, forwarded a loyal memorial,

sentences.zh: 〈時鄧禹承制命嚻為西州大將軍，專制涼州、朔方事。〉

translations.literal: <At that time Deng Yu by edict had appointed Ao western provinces grand general with sole authority over Liang province and Shuofang affairs.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Deng Yu had vested him with Liang and Shuofang.>

sentences.zh: 建義大將軍朱祐率祭遵與延岑戰於東陽，斬其將張成。

translations.literal: General-in-Chief Who Establishes Righteousness Zhu You led Ji Zun to fight Yan Cen at Dongyang and beheaded his general Zhang Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You and Ji Zun met Yan Cen at Dongyang and killed his general Zhang Cheng.

sentences.zh: 〈東陽，聚名也，故城在今鄧州南。

translations.literal: <Dongyang is a settlement name; its old city is south of Dengzhou today.'
translations.idiomatic: <This Dongyang hamlet south of Dengzhou—

sentences.zh: 臨淮郡復有東陽縣，非此地也。〉

translations.literal: Linhuai commandery also has a Dongyang county; it is not this place.'>
translations.idiomatic: not the Dongyang county on the Huai.>

sentences.zh: 四年春正月甲申，大赦天下。

translations.literal: Fourth year, spring, first month, day jiashen, he greatly amnestied All Under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Jianwu 4 opened with jiashen in the first month and another great amnesty,

sentences.zh: 二月壬子，幸懷。

translations.literal: Second month, day renzi, he visited Huai.
translations.idiomatic: on renzi in the second month he went to Huai,

sentences.zh: 壬申，至自懷。

translations.literal: On day renshen he arrived from Huai.
translations.idiomatic: returning from Huai on renshen.

sentences.zh: 遣右將軍鄧禹率二將軍與延岑戰於武當，破之。

translations.literal: He dispatched Right General Deng Yu leading two generals to fight Yan Cen at Wudang and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu met Yan Cen at Wudang and broke his line,

sentences.zh: 〈武當，縣，屬南陽郡，有武當山，今均州縣也。〉

translations.literal: <Wudang is a county belonging to Nanyang commandery; it has Mount Wudang; it is a Junzhou county today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Wudang in Nanyang—modern Junzhou, Hubei.>

sentences.zh: 夏四月丁巳，幸鄴。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, day dingsi, he visited Ye.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi in the fourth month he traveled to Ye,

sentences.zh: 己巳，進幸臨平。

translations.literal: On day jisi he advanced to visit Linping.
translations.idiomatic: on jisi pushed on to Linping,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬鉅鹿郡，故城在今定州鼓城縣東南。〉

translations.literal: <A county name belonging to Julu commandery; its old city is southeast of Gucheng county in Dingzhou today.'>
translations.idiomatic: <Linping southeast of Gucheng in Hebei.>

sentences.zh: 遣大司馬吳漢擊五校賊於箕山，大破之。

translations.literal: He dispatched Grand Marshal Wu Han to strike the Five Camps bandits at Mount Ji and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han crushed the Five Camps at Ji Mountain,

sentences.zh: 〈吳漢傳曰東郡箕山。〉

translations.literal: <Wu Han's biography says Ji Mountain in Dong commandery.'>
translations.idiomatic: <The Wu Han zhuan places the fight on Ji Mountain in Dong.>

sentences.zh: 五月，進幸元氏。

translations.literal: Fifth month, he advanced to visit Yuanshi.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month he moved on to Yuanshi,

sentences.zh: 辛巳，進幸盧奴。

translations.literal: On day xinsi he advanced to visit Luunu.
translations.idiomatic: on xinsi to Luunu,

sentences.zh: 遣征虜將軍祭遵率四將軍討張豐於涿郡，斬豐。

translations.literal: He dispatched General Who Conquers Captives Ji Zun leading four generals to punish Zhang Feng in Zhuo commandery and beheaded Feng.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Zun executed Zhang Feng in Zhuo,

sentences.zh: 六月辛亥，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: Sixth month, day xinhai, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the sixth month the court was back in Luoyang,

sentences.zh: 七月丁亥，幸譙。

translations.literal: Seventh month, day dinghai, he visited Qiao.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai in the seventh month he rode to Qiao,

sentences.zh: 遣捕虜將軍馬武、偏將軍王霸圍劉紆於垂惠。

translations.literal: He dispatched General Who Captures Captives Ma Wu and deputy general Wang Ba to besiege Liu Yu at Chuihui.
translations.idiomatic: detaching Ma Wu and Wang Ba to invest Liu Yu at Chuihui,

translations.idiomatic: <Chuihui northwest of modern Mengcheng, Anhui.>

sentences.zh: 董憲將賁休以蘭陵城降，憲圍之。

translations.idiomatic: Ben Xiu handed Lucheng to Han, but Dong Xian invested the city anyway,

sentences.zh: 〈《前書》曰賁赫。

translations.literal: <The Former Han History cites the name Ben He.'
translations.idiomatic: <The Han shu parallel cites the minister Ben He,

sentences.zh: 賁音肥，今姓作賁，音奔。

translations.literal: Ben is read fei; today the surname is written with another graph, read ben.
translations.idiomatic: the graph read fei or ben in modern usage,

sentences.zh: 蘭陵，縣，屬東海郡，故城在今沂州丞縣東。〉
